conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 16 years old. I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I can help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me start by asking: **Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** For example, is he enrolled in full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or similar courses at school or college)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is! Alex is currently studying for his A-levels at sixth form college. He's doing really well with it actually.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great to hear that Alex is doing well with his A-levels!

Before I can make a final determination, I need to gather a bit more information about your specific circumstances. 

**Do you live in the UK, and is Alex living with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - we live in Birmingham and Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Are you responsible for Alex?** By this I mean, do you look after him and meet his care needs (such as providing food, clothing, and general day-to-day care)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I take care of everything for him - his food, clothes, school supplies, all of that. He's my responsibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's very helpful.

One final question: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** For example, Alex's other parent or another family member?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me just review the official eligibility rules to make sure I give you an accurate assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for your patience. Based on the information you've provided and the official UK government rules, I can now provide you with a determination on your eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

✅ Alex is 16 years old and studying for his A-levels at sixth form college, which counts as approved education
✅ You live in the UK (Birmingham) and Alex lives with you
✅ You are responsible for Alex's care and meet his day-to-day needs
✅ No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for him

You meet all the eligibility requirements. Child Benefit can continue for young people aged 16-19 who remain in approved education or training, and A-levels at sixth form definitely qualify.

**Important note:** Please be aware that if either you or your partner has an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still claim Child Benefit even if the charge applies.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit through the official government process. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 16 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 16 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 16 years old and currently studying for his A-levels at sixth form college, which qualifies as approved education. According to UK Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16, but eligibility continues if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. You live in the UK (Birmingham), Alex lives with you, you are responsible for him (providing food, clothes, and day-to-day care), and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant lives in the UK with their 16-year-old son Alex, who is in approved education (A-levels at sixth form). The claimant is responsible for Alex's care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility requirements under UK Child Benefit rules are satisfied.
